sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.13 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: The well-known Baer--Suzuki theorem states that if every two conjugates of an element$a$of a finite group$G$generate a finite$p 0.2ex$-sub\-group, then$a$is contained in a normal$p 0.2ex$-sub\-group. Does such a theorem hold in the class of periodic groups for$p>2$? A counterexample is known for$p=2$; see Archive 11.11a).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
